title: T Flip-Flop (from JK Flip-Flop Conversion)
examTip: T Flip-Flop Conversion: Tie J and K inputs of a JK Flip-Flop together to form the T input (J = K = T). The characteristic equation becomes Q(next) = T·Q̄ + T̄·Q. T=0 holds, T=1 toggles.

blocks.label: Equations: J = K = T
sublabels: Q(next) = T·Q̄ + T̄·Q  (Hold if T = 0, Toggle if T = 1)

theory:
A T (Toggle) Flip-Flop can be constructed from a JK Flip-Flop by connecting (tying) the J and K inputs together to a single input terminal, designated as T.

Derivation and Operation:
The characteristic equation of a JK flip-flop is:
Q(next) = J·Q̄ + K̄·Q

By tying J and K together and setting J = K = T, the equation becomes:
Q(next) = T·Q̄ + T̄·Q

Analyzing this equation:
- When T = 0: Q(next) = 0·Q̄ + 1·Q = Q (Hold state, no change)
- When T = 1: Q(next) = 1·Q̄ + 0·Q = Q̄ (Toggle state, output complements on clock pulse)

Thus, the circuit functions exactly as a T flip-flop. This is one of the most common flip-flop conversions because it allows a universal JK flip-flop to be easily converted into a toggle element, which is the fundamental building block for binary counters, frequency dividers, and sequential control logic.

Clock Triggering:
The state transitions occur at the active edge of the clock signal (dynamic indicator '>'). For edge-triggered devices, the toggle or hold operation occurs synchronously with the clock transition.
keyPoints: Tied inputs: J and K inputs of the JK flip-flop are connected to form a single T input.
T = 0 mode: Holds the current state (Q(next) = Q).
T = 1 mode: Toggles the output state (Q(next) = Q̄) on the clock transition.
Simplest conversion: Requires no additional logic gates, only a direct connection.
Applications: Used as a frequency divider (by setting T = 1 permanently) and in ripple/synchronous counters.
useCases: Designing synchronous and asynchronous binary counters (e.g., up/down counters).
Frequency division circuits where the output frequency is half of the input clock frequency.
Control logic where state toggling is required on specific input conditions.
